THRESHOLD: the slot canyons of the Redwall fossil beds
OPENING_MOMENT: the dry-season morning, before the heat
DEADLINE_SIGN: muddy runoff rising across the canyon floor
ROUTE_A_LURE: wind humming through a fluted wall
ROUTE_B_LURE: the drip of a seep spring
ROUTE_C_LURE: the dry rattle of loose scree
ROUTE_C_CHAR: the shadowed undercut, the darkest way
A1_LANDMARK: flecks of quartz in the seam wall
A1_ZONE_HINT: a fissure glowing with mineral light
A1_GATE: a narrow slot as runoff pools
A2_LANDMARK: something huge and ribbed far below
A2_GATE: a fixed rope down the slot
A2_FIND: a giant fossil ribcage, ancient and stone-hard
A2_DETAIL1: a species name lost for ages
A2_DETAIL2: a chalk marker and a coil of rope
B1_LANDMARK2: a ledge above a still plunge-pool
B2_OFFER2: a track to a darker basin
B2_PRIZE2_FIND: a perfect ammonite, spiraled and pale
C1_LANDMARK: a colony of roosting cliff swallows
C1_PRIZE: The Rim Notch onto the Mesa
C2_GATE: the wash rising across the niche floor
C2_OFFER2: a tin of antique survey tokens
C2_PRIZE1_ACT: wipes away the dust; the needle swings true
